text.title: shadcn/ui: Mandatory Rules
text.summary: Non-negotiable rules for vendored ui/ directory, CSS variable tokens, cva() variants, cn() usage, and feature component prefixing.

rule.statement: Treat components/ui/ as vendored. Only add or update files there via `npx shadcn@latest add`. Never edit ui/ files directly. Wrap, do not fork.
rule.rationale: The shadcn CLI may overwrite ui/ files on the next `add --overwrite`. Direct edits are silently lost. Customizations belong in feature components that compose the primitives.

rule.statement: Use HSL channel triplets for all theme tokens in globals.css (e.g. `--primary: 221 83% 53%`). Never write hex, rgb(), or hsl() in globals.css token values.
rule.rationale: shadcn's color system uses bare HSL triplets so Tailwind can apply opacity modifiers (`bg-primary/90`). Hex or rgb() values break the alpha helper syntax.

rule.statement: All visual variants must be defined with `cva()` from class-variance-authority. Never pass long Tailwind strings as component props or build them with template literals.
rule.rationale: cva() produces a type-safe variant API, prevents class conflicts, and integrates with Storybook for automatic variant matrix stories. Template literal class names are purged by JIT.

rule.statement: Import `cn` from `lib/utils` for all className construction. Never use string concatenation or bare clsx without tailwind-merge.
rule.rationale: Tailwind class conflicts (e.g. `p-2 p-4`) silently resolve to whichever declaration wins cascade. tailwind-merge resolves conflicts explicitly; bare concatenation does not.

rule.statement: Place feature-specific components under `components/[feature]/`, never in `components/ui/`. Name them with the feature prefix: `billing/PlanCard.tsx`, not `ui/PlanCard.tsx`.
rule.rationale: Mixing feature components into ui/ creates merge conflicts on every `shadcn add` run and blurs the vendored/owned boundary that the CI guard enforces.

rule.statement: Ensure `components.json` aliases (`@/components`, `@/lib/utils`) match `tsconfig.json` path mappings. Run this sync check after any shadcn init or CLI upgrade.
rule.rationale: CLI-generated imports use path aliases. Mismatched tsconfig paths produce silent import failures — TypeScript resolves the type but the bundler cannot find the file.

rule.statement: Check for `"use client"` at the top of every shadcn component before using it in a Next.js App Router RSC tree. Do not add shadcn components to RSC files without this check.
rule.rationale: Most shadcn components use React hooks and require the client boundary. Missing `"use client"` produces cryptic `ReactCurrentDispatcher` hydration errors in Next.js App Router.
